LAW AND MOTION 302, NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE ISSUE OF NOTICE WAS DISCUSSED AND THE COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: ISSUANCE OF A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT HE IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE RECORD REFLECTS THAT PLAINTIFF IS AN EXPERIENCED REAL ESTATE BROKER AND THE LOAN DOCUMENTS MAKE CLEAR THAT THE INTEREST IS VARIABLE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE LOAN WAS PROCURED BY FRAUD. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Order To Show Cause Re Issuance Of A Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Martin Eng (246.1111) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona

No other appearances on this matter. The issue of notice was discussed and the Court denied plaintiff's request for a continuance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: ISSUANCE OF A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is denied. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate that he is likely to prevail on the merits. The record reflects that plaintiff is an experienced real estate broker and the loan documents make clear that the interest is variable. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate that the loan was procured by fraud. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
